_comment: This file is the machine-readable record of the pinned Bedrock model matrix and
the on-demand quotas granted for each model in this account/region pair.
It is referenced by ARCHITECTURE.md (Model-selection policy) and consumed by the
eval harness to derive max_eval_parallelism and enforce rate-limiting.
1. Verify quotas in AWS Console → Service Quotas → Amazon Bedrock.
2. Update granted_tpm, granted_rpm, and recertification.recertified_at below.
4. Record the gold_set_run_id from the certifying eval run in recertification.
5. Open a PR; this file is governed by CODEOWNERS (admin / GC approval required).
Quarterly recertification: re-run the eval harness gold set, confirm quota figures
are still accurate, update recertification.recertified_at and gold_set_run_id.

_note: Quarterly eval gate — updated on every recertification.
gold_set_run_id: reference to the eval run ID that certified this policy version.
Use the sentinel 'bootstrap' for the initial policy prior to a full gold-set run.
See ARCHITECTURE.md → Model-selection policy → Quarterly recertification.

_quota_note: AWS default on-demand quota for Claude Opus 4.8 (anthropic.claude-opus-4-8)
in us-east-1 as of 2026-06-13: 40,000 TPM / 50 RPM (Standard tier).
Verify via: AWS Console → Service Quotas → Amazon Bedrock →
'Tokens per minute for claude-opus-4-8 on-demand throughput' and
'Requests per minute for claude-opus-4-8 on-demand throughput'.
A quota increase request is on file for the eval harness workload.
If granted_tpm changes, re-derive review_throughput_ceiling and
AWS default on-demand quota for Claude Sonnet 4.6 (anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6)
in us-east-1 as of 2026-06-13: 100,000 TPM / 200 RPM (Standard tier).
Sonnet 4.6 has a higher default quota than Opus 4.8 — the critic pass is
cheaper AND less quota-constrained, reinforcing the two-model architecture.
Verify via: AWS Console → Service Quotas → Amazon Bedrock.
Embedding model quota is not the bottleneck for review throughput.
A change to the embedding model requires admin (GC) approval and
produces a new corpus_snapshot_version — see ARCHITECTURE.md.

_derivation: Bottleneck: Opus 4.8 primary pass (40,000 TPM, 50 RPM).
Per-review primary-pass token consumption (worst case, uncached):
input:  60,000 tokens (playbook ~30K + diff ~15K + retrieved context ~15K)
output:  8,000 tokens
total:  68,000 tokens per primary call.
Token-budget ceiling: floor(40,000 TPM / 68,000 tokens/review) ≈ 0.59 reviews/min
→ ≈ 35 reviews/hour (token-limited at 40K TPM).
RPM ceiling: 50 RPM primary calls / 1 primary call per review = 50 reviews/min
→ RPM is NOT the binding constraint at this call volume.
Prompt-cache effect: playbook block (~30K tokens) caches across back-to-back
runs; effective uncached input per cached review ≈ 30,000 tokens.
Cached throughput ceiling: floor(40,000 / (30,000+8,000)) ≈ 1.05 reviews/min
→ ≈ 63 reviews/hour when cache is warm.
Production volume (2–7/day) is safely under either ceiling.
The eval harness (39 cases × 2 passes × N stochastic runs) is the stress case.
Eval harness worst case (issue #17):
gold_set_cases: 39
passes_per_case: 2  (primary + critic)
stochastic_runs: 3  (default; configurable via EVAL_STOCHASTIC_RUNS)
tokens_per_primary_call: ~68,000 (uncached)
tokens_per_critic_call:  ~75,000 (70K input + 5K output)
Total primary-call tokens per full eval run (uncached):
39 cases × 3 runs × 68,000 = 7,956,000 tokens
At 40K TPM this takes ~199 minutes if sequential.
With prompt-cache warm on playbook block:
effective tokens/primary call ≈ 38,000
Max parallelism to stay under 40K TPM quota:
Each parallel runner issues one call every ~102s (68K / 40K TPM × 60).
Two parallel runners: 136K tokens/min → exceeds quota → ThrottlingException.
Safe parallelism = 1 runner at a time per 40K-TPM account.
Exception: if quota is increased, update max_runners below.
Rate-limiting strategy: the eval harness serializes primary-pass calls
(one in-flight Opus call at a time) and uses a token-bucket with a
40K TPM refill rate.  Critic calls (Sonnet 4.6, 100K TPM) can run
concurrently with the NEXT primary call but are also token-bucket limited.
This also maximizes prompt-cache hit rate on back-to-back runs.
max_eval_parallelism.rate_limit_strategy: token-bucket — serialize primary-pass calls; token-bucket-limit critic calls

_note: ThrottlingException retries from the pipeline or the eval harness must NOT
fire the 'Bedrock invocation errors > 0' CloudWatch alarm.  They are an
expected operational signal when approaching quota, handled by exponential
backoff retry, and must be tracked as a separate 'ThrottlingException retries'
Alarm split (see ARCHITECTURE.md → Observability and RUNBOOK.md → Incident response):
- 'Bedrock invocation errors > 0'  : fires on non-throttle errors only
These indicate a genuine problem requiring human investigation.
- 'Bedrock throttle retries > N'   : fires when ThrottlingException retry
count exceeds N in a 5-minute window, indicating sustained quota pressure.
Informational by default; escalated if retries cause SLA misses.
Error alarm: filter on Bedrock errorCode NOT IN ('ThrottlingException')
Throttle alarm: filter on Bedrock errorCode = 'ThrottlingException'
